skill.name: req-change-workflow
skill.description: Guides requirement and feature changes through a gated workflow: clarify scope, confirm current behavior, assess impact, design the change, implement small diffs, validate regressions, and record decisions.
skill.summary: Run controlled requirement changes with scope, impact, validation, and decision records.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found they are false positives from Markdown inline code, bilingual prose, and templates. The only confirmed risk is a local Bash helper that executes rg for project impact scanning; it is scoped to local files and does not show exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Local Search Script Executes ripgrep
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Bash helper that checks for rg and runs rg against the local project tree. Arguments are passed through arrays and there is no network access, credential access, or shell eval, so this is a legitimate workflow helper with low operational risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly invokes local command-line tooling, but the command and arguments are structured. No evidence found of command injection, network transfer, or secret collection.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick and weak cryptography matches occur in Markdown prose, inline file names, template references, and workflow examples. No evidence found of Ruby execution, weak cryptographic APIs, encoded payloads, malware behavior, or prompt injection text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible Markdown guidance and inline paths. They do not contain executable Ruby, cryptographic code, override instructions, or hidden payloads.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristic Is Natural Language Content
low_findings.description: The entropy alerts point to Chinese and bilingual Markdown templates, not binary data or encrypted content. The files are readable checklist and change-brief text with no obfuscated instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms normal Markdown structure and readable bilingual content. No evidence found of encoded blobs, packed scripts, or encrypted payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Search Script Executes ripgrep
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a Bash helper that checks for rg and runs rg against the local project tree. Arguments are passed through arrays and there is no network access, credential access, or shell eval, so this is a legitimate workflow helper with low operational risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly invokes local command-line tooling, but the command and arguments are structured. No evidence found of command injection, network transfer, or secret collection.

content.value_statement: Requirement changes often expand beyond their intended scope and create unclear regression risk. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a gated workflow for scoping, impact review, validation, and decision records.
actual_capabilities: Turns a requested feature change into a scoped change brief with acceptance criteria.
Guides codebase baseline review before implementation begins.
Identifies likely affected Chrome extension files and workflow entrypoints.
Creates impact, risk, rollback, and validation plans before edits.
Runs a fixed regression checklist for extension UI, service worker, auth, and data flows.
Prompts maintainers to record design decisions and rejected alternatives.
limitations: It does not implement changes before the user approves the proposed design.
It assumes the assistant can read the target repository and run local tools when permitted.
Its regression checklist is tailored to Chrome extension projects and may need adaptation.
It does not replace product ownership decisions or manual browser verification.

use_cases.title: Control a Chrome extension feature change
use_cases.description: Scope a change that touches manifest, service worker, storage, auth, or UI before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review change impact before coding
use_cases.description: Map current behavior, affected files, risks, and rollback needs for a requested workflow adjustment.

use_cases.title: Create repeatable validation records
use_cases.description: Capture tested checklist items, observed results, and design decisions for future maintainers.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a Small Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the req-change-workflow skill. Help me turn this requested change into a short change brief with target behavior, out-of-scope items, and acceptance criteria: [describe change].

prompt_templates.title: Assess Current Behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Existing code must be understood before editing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the req-change-workflow skill. Inspect the current implementation for [feature or flow], summarize the real entrypoints and data flow, then list the files that matter and why.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Risky Extension Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Change may affect auth, storage, service worker, or manifest behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the req-change-workflow skill. Build an impact and rollback plan for changing [workflow]. Include risks, manual validation steps, and what must stay unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Run the Full Gated Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request from scoping through documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the req-change-workflow skill end to end for [change request]. Do not edit code until I approve the design. After implementation, run the regression checklist and draft a decision log entry.

output_examples.input: Change the login refresh behavior without changing the OAuth scope.
output: A change brief that states the target behavior and keeps OAuth scope out of scope.
A current behavior summary covering the service worker, auth flow, and affected UI entrypoints.
A validation plan that includes reload, login, refresh, failure handling, and rollback checks.

output_examples.input: Adjust import behavior but avoid breaking old exported data.
output: An impact list focused on storage compatibility, import entrypoints, and export test pages.
Acceptance criteria for old data, new data, failure messages, and rollback expectations.
A decision log entry explaining the selected compatibility strategy.

output_examples.input: Refactor a scattered UI flow into one module.
output: A proposed new flow with state boundaries and edge cases.
A minimal file change budget for the refactor.
A regression record showing which UI and manual checklist items were tested.

best_practices: Approve acceptance criteria, affected files, and design before editing code.
Use the impact scan only as a starting point, then read the relevant files directly.
Record manual validation results and rollback notes after every meaningful change.
anti_patterns: Starting implementation before the current behavior is confirmed from code.
Expanding the file list without updating the risk and rollback plan.
Treating a manual regression checklist as optional after touching extension entrypoints.

faq.question: What kind of projects is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for existing codebases with feature changes that need scope control, impact review, and regression validation.

faq.question: Is this only for Chrome extensions?
faq.answer: No. The workflow is general, but the included checklist and examples are optimized for Chrome extension projects.

faq.question: Will the assistant edit code immediately?
faq.answer: No. The workflow requires user approval of the acceptance criteria, affected files, and proposed design before implementation.

faq.question: What artifacts does it produce?
faq.answer: It produces a change brief, current behavior summary, impact and rollback plan, validation record, and decision log entry.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide local commands when permitted, but the built-in checklist includes manual browser validation steps.

faq.question: How should teams adapt it?
faq.answer: Teams should update the checklist with their real entrypoints, test pages, storage rules, and release process.
